### Implementation details (Straightforward Dijkstra)

1. Precalculate $closest\_idx$. $O(N)$.
2. For each query $[L, R]$:
    a. Initialize distances $D$. $D[L]=0$.
    b. Use a Min-Priority Queue storing (cost, index).
    c. While PQ is not empty:
        i. Extract $(d, x)$.
        ii. If $d > D[x]$, continue.
        iii. Check neighbors $y=0$ to $N-1$:
            Calculate cost $C(x \to y)$.
            If $y = closest(x)$: Cost is $\min(C(x, y), 1)$.
            If $y \neq closest(x)$: Cost is $C(x, y)$.
            
            If $D[x] + \text{Cost}(x \to y) < D[y]$:
